refined_fr.refined_fr: Provide detailed worksite area analysis including terrain mapping, obstacle identification, and work-zone representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing into specific analysis tasks clarifies requirements and supports targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by generating a worksite model/map that accurately represents terrain features, obstacles, and designated work zones, validated against ground truth data.
sub_frs: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area
Identify and map obstacles within the worksite
Represent designated work zones within the worksite model

candidate_dps: Terrain analysis algorithm using LIDAR and sensor fusion
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrated worksite modeling system combining terrain analysis, obstacle detection, and work-zone representation algorithms

potential_sources: Data dependencies between terrain and obstacle analysis
coupling_check.notes: The integrated system logically groups related analysis functions minimizing cross-module dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensor data (LIDAR, cameras)
Risk of data latency affecting real-time model accuracy
High safety criticality requires robust fault detection
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing obstacle detection separately if sensor data processing load is high.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized processing and modeling of worksite data including terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data streams for terrain and obstacle analysis

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed worksite model data to planning and execution modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange with remote monitoring and control systems

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as core modeling logic is centralized here; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module primarily executes commands; limited containment of modeling logic.
Teleoperation focuses on control interfaces, not modeling.
Safety Services consume alerts but do not perform modeling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing worksite modeling in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership and interface management.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottleneck if all modeling functions centralized
Integration complexity with sensor data sources
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor performance and consider splitting heavy sensor processing into dedicated submodules within NGHE Solution if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support the assignment; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by validating the generated worksite model against known site data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of modeling functions to NGHE Solution module.

recommended_revisions: Evaluate sensor data processing load to avoid performance bottlenecks.
Ensure robust interface definitions for Telemetry and Tasking.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the requirements and constraints with acceptable risk and clear ownership.
next_level_sub_frs: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area
Identify and map obstacles within the worksite
Represent designated work zones within the worksite model

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Accurate and timely sensor data availability
Centralized processing capability within NGHE Solution
evidence_needed: Sensor data quality and latency metrics
Performance benchmarks of NGHE Solution processing
